headline.purpose: Major page headings with restrained product contrast.

metadata.purpose: Commands, digests, versions, registry names, and timestamps.

shadows.name: control-edge
shadows.purpose: A slight physical edge for outline controls and inputs.

shadows.name: surface-low
shadows.purpose: Standalone cards only when tonal separation is insufficient.

motion.name: press
motion.purpose: Immediate tactile confirmation for pressable controls.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The decisive action control: compact, high contrast, and tactile.

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: A bounded secondary action that stays subordinate to the primary.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline{height:36px;padding:8px 16px;border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:8px;background:var(--background);color:var(--foreground);font:500 14px/1.25 'Inter Variable',sans-serif;box-shadow:0 1px 2px rgb(0 0 0/.05);transition:background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(.23,1,.32,1),transform 150ms cubic-bezier(.23,1,.32,1)}.ds-btn-outline:hover{background:var(--accent)}.ds-btn-outline:active{transform:scale(.97)}.ds-btn-outline:focus-visible{outline:3px solid color-mix(in oklab,var(--ring) 50%,transparent);outline-offset:2px}

components.name: Ledger Input
components.description: A compact field with a visible boundary and synchronized focus ring.
components.css: .ds-input{box-sizing:border-box;width:100%;height:36px;padding:4px 12px;border:1px solid var(--input);border-radius:8px;background:transparent;color:var(--foreground);font:400 14px/1.25 'Inter Variable',sans-serif;box-shadow:0 1px 2px rgb(0 0 0/.05);transition:border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(.23,1,.32,1),box-shadow 150ms cubic-bezier(.23,1,.32,1)}.ds-input::placeholder{color:var(--muted-foreground)}.ds-input:focus{outline:0;border-color:var(--ring);box-shadow:0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklab,var(--ring) 50%,transparent)}

components.name: Catalog Navigation
components.description: Muted at rest, full ink when active; navigation does not compete with catalog content.
components.html: ComponentsDocs

components.name: Dashboard Page Header
components.description: The fixed dashboard title, description, icon, and action composition.
components.html:
Collections

Group your components into installable kits.
components.css: .ds-page-header{display:flex;align-items:flex-start;justify-content:space-between;gap:16px;color:var(--foreground);font-family:'Inter Variable',sans-serif}.ds-page-header h1{margin:0;font-size:24px;line-height:1.25;font-weight:600}.ds-page-header p{max-width:48rem;margin:4px 0 0;color:var(--muted-foreground);font-size:14px;line-height:1.45}.ds-page-header button{height:32px;padding:6px 12px;border:0;border-radius:8px;background:var(--primary);color:var(--primary-foreground);font:500 14px/1 'Inter Variable',sans-serif}.ds-page-header button:active{transform:scale(.97)}.ds-page-header button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid color-mix(in oklab,var(--ring) 50%,transparent);outline-offset:2px}

components.name: Empty State
components.description: A teaching state for product regions without data or matching results.
components.html:
No collections yet

Bundle related components and users can install the whole set together.

components.name: Price Ticket
components.description: The signature paid-exchange marker; never evidence or approval.
components.css: .ds-price-ticket{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;padding:4px 14px;border-radius:5px;background:var(--ticket);color:var(--ticket-foreground);font:600 11px/1.2 ui-monospace,monospace;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums;mask:radial-gradient(3.5px at 0 50%,#0000 97%,#000 100%) left 50%/51% 100% no-repeat,radial-gradient(3.5px at 100% 50%,#0000 97%,#000 100%) right 50%/51% 100% no-repeat}

components.name: Evidence Record
components.description: A scoped positive record with explicit copy and a non-color status icon.
components.html: Content integrityExact files match the published digest

narrative.overview: Modulora looks like a public record that is meant to be used, not admired from a distance. Graphite surfaces organize dense component, evidence, licensing, install, and earnings data. Crisp boundaries and compact controls make every action feel accountable. The interface is bold through contrast and specificity rather than oversized type, decoration, or spectacle. The system is tactile and exact. Controls visibly answer presses, selected filters are unmistakable, and meaningful records sit beside the decisions they support.
keyCharacteristics: Dark-first graphite ledger with a complete light inverse.
Compact, readable information density built around an 8px spacing rhythm.
Inter for interface clarity; monospace only for commands, digests, versions, and machine records.
Amber marks priced exchange; emerald marks successful records and earnings.
Motion is short feedback or state explanation, never page-load choreography.
Authorship, association, similarity, and review scope remain visible at the point of discovery.

rules.name: The Receipt Rule
rules.body: Amber describes money; emerald describes a completed or successful record. Neither color is a generic highlight.

rules.name: The Scoped Trust Rule
rules.body: Approval and evidence always include a label or backing record. Never turn semantic color into a universal verified claim.

rules.name: The Authorship Rule
rules.body: Creator identity and original-source attribution use full text labels and traceable records. Similarity warnings use neutral review language; they never borrow success green or approval styling.

rules.name: The Machine Record Rule
rules.body: Monospace means the user can copy, compare, or verify the value. Never use it merely to make the product look technical.

rules.name: The Tone-First Rule
rules.body: If a border and a wide soft shadow appear on the same card, the hierarchy is wrong.

dos: Do place provenance, license, fulfillment, price, and evidence limitations beside install and purchase decisions.
Do use the graphite tonal ladder before adding borders or shadows.
Do reserve Ticket Amber for money and Receipt Emerald for successful records and earnings.
Do show original source, creator authorization, review scope, and report/dispute access beside component identity.
Do label similarity as a signal for review, never as an automated verdict.
donts: Don't resemble a generic SaaS template: no decorative purple gradients, glass-card surfaces, interchangeable dashboard composition, or vague AI-era copy.
Don't use a generic verified badge.
Don't let promotion, Plus, price, or visual emphasis imply approval, safety, review priority, or rank quality.
Don't imply that listing review proves ownership, originality, security, or freedom from plagiarism.
Don't create accounts, listings, creator profiles, imported previews, or apparent brand associations without the creator's affirmative authorization.
